items.title: DOJ Seeks Forfeiture of $3.4M in USDT Tied to Ethereum Investment Scam
items.summary: Federal prosecutors in Massachusetts have moved to forfeit 3.44 million USDT linked to an alleged text-based crypto investment fraud.

items.title: Bitcoin drops on Iran war uncertainty, AI tokens jump
items.summary: BTC traded near $69,500 after failing to hold $71,000 as the Iran war kept markets cautious. AI tokens including ICP, FET outperformed on strong retail demand.

items.title: US seeks forfeiture of $3.4M in USDt tied to crypto investment scam
items.summary: US prosecutors moved to forfeit $3.44 million in USDt allegedly tied to a crypto investment scam that tricked victims into sending Ether to wallets controlled by fraudsters.

items.title: Aave wstETH glitch forces $27M in liquidations and compensation
items.summary: Capo, an external oracle solution used by Aave, triggered around $27 million in liquidations after a pricing glitch, but the lending platform said it is stepping in to cover the losses.

items.title: Oracle jumps 11% premarket as AI demand challenges 'SaaS apocalypse' fears
items.summary: Cloud and AI growth beat expectations while investors grow less worried about Oracle’s debt plans and software disruption.

items.title: Bitcoin ETFs add $251M as Goldman Sachs tops XRP ETF holders
items.summary: US spot Bitcoin ETFs continued their March rally, with cumulative monthly inflows reaching $1.56 billion, while XRP ETF selling eased as top holders were revealed.

items.title: Tokenized assets climb to $23.6B as investors seek always-on markets
items.summary: Tokenized real-world assets have surged 66% in 2026, with funds, gold and equities driving growth across public blockchains.

items.title: Bitcoin faces ‘highly volatile’ setup as bulls eye return to $80K by month-end
items.summary: Bitcoin open interest sparked a prediction of high BTC price volatility to come as $70,000 remained the bulls' key reclaim level.

items.title: Brera board approves Solmate pivot, cuts soccer teams to focus on Solana
items.summary: Nasdaq-listed Brera plans to rebrand as Solmate, wind down two soccer teams and propose a 10-for-1 reverse stock split as it pivots toward Solana.

items.title: Coinbase-backed AI payments protocol wants to fix micropayment but demand is just not there yet
items.summary: Agentic commerce holds promise, but data shows that x402 is still in the trial phase

items.title: Next week could spice things up for bitcoin as seven central banks face an inflation test
items.summary: Seven major central banks, including the Federal Reserve, will issue rate decisions next week just as war-driven oil price spikes raise fresh concerns about global inflation.

items.title: Senate Democrats push ban on prediction market bets tied to war and death
items.summary: Senate Democrats' bill would write the prohibition into federal law even as the CFTC shifts toward a more permissive stance on event contracts.

items.title: DAOs aren't doomed, they just need to evolve: Aave founder
items.summary: A proposal to transfer control of Aave’s brand assets and intellectual property to its DAO failed in January, prompting debate over the protocol’s long-term direction.

items.title: ‘Adoption paradox’ in effect as Ether lags while network activity surges
items.summary: Data shows that Ether price dynamics are being driven by capital flows rather than network activity growth, said CryptoQuant analyst Julio Moreno.

items.title: Bitcoin will need 17% of ‘store of value’ market to hit $1M: Bitwise
items.summary: Bitcoin doesn't need to take 50% of gold's market share to reach one million dollars per coin if one is looking into the next 10 years, argues Bitwise's Matt Hougan.

items.title: Crypto, banks need to be a ‘bit unhappy’ for bill to advance: Senator
items.summary: Senator Angela Alsobrooks says she’s working on a proposal to move a key crypto bill forward, but crypto and the banks will have to compromise.

items.summary: The company plans to obtain the license through the acquisition of BC Payments Australia, expanding its regulated footprint to over 75 licenses worldwide.

items.title: Bitcoin steady above $70,000 as IEA proposes largest-ever oil reserve release
items.summary: The largest cryptocurrency gained 7% from Monday's lows as energy price fears eased, with Asian equities rising 1.8% and Brent crude dropping below $90 for the first time since the war began.

items.title: XRP Ledger transactions jump to 2.7M as price stays rangebound
items.summary: Traders are watching whether support near $1.34 holds after repeated rejection near $1.44.

items.title: Bitcoin traders bet on a rally above $80,000
items.summary: Many traders expect bitcoin to recover toward the $80K level between June and September, Derive said.

items.title: Democrats Introduce Bill to Ban Prediction Market Contracts on War and Death
items.summary: The DEATH BETS Act would prohibit CFTC-registered exchanges from listing contracts tied to terrorism, assassination, war, or death.

items.title: Dogecoin zooms as Elon Musk announces X Money launch date for April
items.summary: The payments app offers 6% yield on balances and peer-to-peer transfers inside X, competing directly with the same demand stablecoin products are targeting. No mention of dogecoin or any crypto functionality was included.

items.title: Crypto is just finance with new plumbing: Australia’s ASIC fintech chief
items.summary: New regulatory frameworks weren’t needed when financial infrastructure shifted from paper to electronic records, so it isn't needed for blockchain either, argues ASIC’s Rhys Bollen.

items.title: Ethereum's on fire with record activity, but ether price and blockchain fees lag
items.summary: Capital outflows, even as activity surges across Ethereum’s ecosystem, highlight the growing disconnect between usage growth and ETH’s market performance, a CryptoQuant report shows.

items.summary: Experts warn that encrypted chats could face a “harvest now, decrypt later” risk as quantum computing advances.

items.summary: The prediction markets platform argued for an injunction against Ohio authorities, claiming that federal commodities laws superseded state laws on sports event contracts.
